title: Week 27
summary:
Capesize
Friday ended on an upbeat note after a week of rates in the East drifting lower. The volume of fixing had been significant on the West Australia/Qingdao run, but rates Thursday dipped to $5.05 or thereabout. Today the market turned up with rumours $5.20 was concluded for 16-20 July and some suggesting more.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: Friday ended on an upbeat note after a week of rates in the East drifting lower. The volume of fixing had been significant on the West Australia/Qingdao run, but rates Thursday dipped to $5.05 or thereabout.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: A tight supply of tonnage in the North Atlantic prompted a rise in rates with still cargoes there to be fixed. Charterers with cargoes into the US in some cases conceded fixing a couple of legs with an eco kamsarmax booked for two laden legs basis Ghent for a relatively long duration at $10,000daily.

children.title: Supramax / Handysize
children.summary: A week of relatively modest but steady gains within the Atlantic. In the US Gulf, a nice 2013 built 61,400 dwt ultramax was booked for a front-haul trip at close to $17,000 daily and a 2009 built 55,500 dwt ship was fixed for a similar run at $15,500 daily.
